skills.name: agent-legibility-review
skills.purpose: Reviews whether a repository is easy for future AI coding agents to find, understand, modify, and safely evolve.
triggers: review a repository for agent navigability
find hidden conventions, duplicated authority, ambiguous ownership, or agent risk hotspots
evaluate whether future Codex work can safely modify a repository
do_not_use_for: ordinary code-style review
framework-specific review
bug, performance, security, clean-code, maintainability, or generic architecture review unless agent legibility is explicitly requested
validation_expectations: inspect repository evidence before reporting findings
run a disproof pass against candidate findings
cite concrete files, modules, docs, tests, or repo-wide structure
skills.portability_notes: Portable review workflow, but findings must stay grounded in the target repository's actual docs, tests, code, and conventions.

skills.name: ai-career-signal-researcher
skills.purpose: Researches AI systems engineering career, market, infrastructure, and portfolio signals for a backend engineer moving into AI systems work.
triggers: research AI systems engineering trends
compare AI infrastructure, agents, RAG, evals, MCP, or observability demand
find AI engineering career signals or content opportunities
do_not_use_for: generic career advice detached from evidence
popularity-only YouTube or social trend summaries
recommendations that do not connect back to backend engineering or production AI systems
validation_expectations: verify live sources when current market data matters
separate evidence strength from interpretation
use transcripts or substantive technical evidence instead of popularity metrics when assessing engineering depth
skills.portability_notes: Personal-skill framing is optimized for a backend engineer moving into AI systems engineering. The YouTube collector requires SERPAPI_KEY and SUPADATA_API_KEY, while other research can use whatever live sources are available.

skills.name: architecture-review
skills.purpose: Reviews repository architecture from local evidence, focusing on concept ownership, authority, boundaries, drift, and change surface.
triggers: assess concept ownership or authoritative sources
review module boundaries, architecture decisions, or architecture drift
plan phased architecture refactors grounded in local evidence
do_not_use_for: ordinary code review
testing review
implementation review
framework or style review
narrow bug diagnosis unless architecture assessment is explicitly requested
validation_expectations: map architecture evidence before recommendations
attempt to disprove each candidate finding
cite concrete files, modules, docs, configs, tests, or runtime wiring
skills.portability_notes: Portable review workflow. It should not be used to impose a desired architecture that is not supported by the target repository's current evidence.

skills.name: brand-deal-researcher
skills.purpose: Turns sponsorship and paid-promotion emails into researched, deduplicated, prioritized brand lead lists.
triggers: scan Gmail or email exports for paid promotion offers
research sponsorship, brand deal, creator partnership, affiliate, or paid collaboration leads
create a table, spreadsheet, or document of brand leads
do_not_use_for: newsletters, generic product marketing, recruiting, support, or PR-only emails with no paid angle
emails asking the creator or company to pay for promotion
raw private email dumps
primary_outputs: spreadsheet or document artifact when requested
validation_expectations: state the exact email date range and keyword families searched
research shortlisted brands using official or credible current sources
mark uncertain brands as unclear or needing verification
skills.portability_notes: Personal workflow depends on access to the user's Gmail or email exports and should avoid copying private email text beyond short evidence snippets.

skills.name: buffer-publisher
skills.purpose: Operates Buffer accounts, organizations, channels, ideas, drafts, queues, scheduled posts, and post mutations through Buffer's GraphQL API.
triggers: draft, schedule, queue, publish, edit, delete, review, list, or manage Buffer social posts
inspect connected Buffer account, organization, channel, queue, post, or daily-limit state
save social content as a Buffer Idea or draft
do_not_use_for: immediate publishing without explicit user request
custom scheduling before converting the requested local time to UTC
direct local media upload because Buffer post assets require reachable URLs
editing or deleting posts without repeating the target post ID and receiving confirmation
primary_outputs: draft, queued, scheduled, or published posts
validation_expectations: confirm account, organization, and channel before writes
run preview-post before post creation unless the user explicitly asked for immediate creation
show preflight details and require explicit confirmation for publish-affecting or destructive actions
skills.portability_notes: Tool-specific personal workflow. Requires BUFFER_API_KEY or the configured local keychain item and must never print or commit the API key.

skills.name: database-access-audit
skills.purpose: Performs a read-only database access audit for batch operations, looped DB I/O, bulk writes, transactions, scope isolation, rowcount, constraints, and indexes.
triggers: audit database access patterns
review batch operations, N+1 queries, bulk writes, attach/detach logic, transactions, or scope predicates
check rowcount, concurrency, constraint, or index risks across a stack
do_not_use_for: production database connections
migrations, destructive commands, write scripts, data repair scripts, benchmark jobs, load tests, or backfills without explicit authorization
broad DAL or repository rewrites unless explicitly requested
validation_expectations: default to static, read-only repository inspection
distinguish query, connection, transaction, round trip, statement, row mutation, flush, commit, and object mutation
include disproof evidence and validation needed for findings
skills.portability_notes: Portable across stacks, but the audit must discover project-specific database primitives before applying generic search terms.

skills.name: decision-trace-writer
skills.purpose: Writes durable local agent-facing traces for stable engineering decisions, findings, review outcomes, and user-clarified constraints.
triggers: preserve stable engineering decision context for future Codex runs
record architecture tradeoffs, debugging findings, review outcomes, or clarified constraints
update an existing decision trace after implementation or validation changes
do_not_use_for: routine implementation details with no reusable decision
transient task plans, TODO lists, meeting notes, brainstorming artifacts, or chat recaps
unresolved investigations where facts are still changing
formal ADRs intended for human readers
primary_outputs: updated trace with current status and validation
small decision record when a compact trace is enough
validation_expectations: apply the worth-recording gate before writing
inspect local evidence and existing trace conventions before choosing placement
verify the trace file exists, is readable, includes concrete references, and has accurate status
skills.portability_notes: Personal agent-memory workflow. Placement must follow the target repository's existing durable-memory convention and should avoid committing ignored or private agent-facing traces unless explicitly requested.

skills.name: diagnose
skills.purpose: Runs a disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions before fixing them.
triggers: diagnose this
debug this
a bug is broken, throwing, failing, or regressing
performance regression investigation
do_not_use_for: speculative fixes without reproduction
routine implementation that does not require diagnosis
broad refactors before the failure mode is understood
primary_outputs: fix and regression validation when requested
validation_expectations: reproduce or bound the failure before fixing
test hypotheses one at a time
convert the minimized repro into a regression check when feasible
skills.portability_notes: Portable diagnosis loop, but concrete commands, fixtures, and test seams must be discovered from the target repository.

skills.name: excalidraw-diagrams
skills.purpose: Creates polished editable Excalidraw diagrams from text descriptions and can export shareable Excalidraw URLs.
triggers: create a diagram, flowchart, architecture diagram, decision tree, process flow, data flow, or system overview
draw this
diagram this
visualize this
make a chart
do_not_use_for: Mermaid input passed directly to excalidraw-cli
sequence diagrams, Gantt charts, pie charts, or bar charts where Mermaid or another charting tool is a better fit
deleting generated files unless the user asks or the work is in a temporary scratch location
primary_outputs: Markdown index for multi-file outputs when useful
validation_expectations: inspect excalidraw-cli supported fields when exact element properties matter
generate the .excalidraw artifact from JSON elements
produce or report preview status and return the share URL when exported
skills.portability_notes: Tool-specific workflow that assumes excalidraw-cli is installed and, for local previews, may depend on platform-specific preview tooling.

skills.name: genmedia
skills.purpose: Uses the genmedia CLI to discover, inspect, run, upload to, and manage fal.ai model endpoints from the terminal.
triggers: genmedia
fal CLI
search models
run a model
fetch schema
check pricing
upload to fal
queue async job
track request
direct interaction with the fal.ai endpoint catalog
do_not_use_for: inventing endpoint IDs
running endpoints before inspecting schema
saving credentials in skill files, scripts, logs, generated docs, or final answers
using pretty output where an agent needs structured JSON
primary_outputs: fal.ai model search and schema results
async job status or result data
validation_expectations: verify endpoint IDs with genmedia models
inspect schema before running unfamiliar endpoints
use --json for agent-readable output and --download for generated media
skills.portability_notes: Personal tool workflow requiring genmedia setup and fal credentials via genmedia setup, FAL_KEY, or local environment. Optional mini app paths are placeholders that must be adapted locally.

skills.name: grill-me
skills.purpose: Pressure-tests ideas, plans, architecture, implementations, PRs, design docs, migrations, rollouts, and technical decisions with direct senior-engineer critique.
triggers: challenge, critique, review, pressure-test, or grill an idea
stress-test a plan, architecture, implementation, PR, design doc, migration, rollout, or technical decision
identify weak assumptions, tradeoffs, risks, and concrete next actions
do_not_use_for: simple factual questions
ordinary code explanation
tasks where the user clearly wants direct implementation instead of critique
validation_expectations: ground critique in available evidence when evidence exists
attempt to disprove objections before presenting them
avoid manufacturing criticism when the plan is supported
skills.portability_notes: Portable critique workflow. It may operate from user-provided context alone or from repository evidence when available.

skills.name: grill-with-docs
skills.purpose: Stress-tests architecture, plans, domain models, workflow semantics, or durable repo knowledge against local repository docs and conventions.
triggers: pressure-test a plan against repository docs
challenge architecture, domain model, workflow semantics, or durable repo knowledge
update repo-local context such as .manifest files, CONTEXT files, or ADRs after stable findings
do_not_use_for: ordinary bug fixes
small code edits
routine PR review
one-off code tracing unless architectural pressure-testing is explicitly requested
public product docs when the knowledge is only personal agent context
primary_outputs: updated repo-local context, CONTEXT files, or ADR-shaped notes when appropriate
validation_expectations: read local docs and implementation evidence before preserving claims
downgrade or remove objections invalidated by repository evidence
persist only stable, validated knowledge
skills.portability_notes: Personal repository-knowledge workflow. It should discover local documentation conventions instead of assuming .manifest, CONTEXT, or ADR paths.

skills.name: paper-deck-style
skills.purpose: Guides creation or extension of minimal, spacious, high-end slide designs for Paper boards.
triggers: create or extend a Paper slide deck
make a Paper presentation feel minimal, spacious, premium, and keynote-like
match or refine Paper slide visual hierarchy
do_not_use_for: crowded text-heavy slide layouts
inventing a new deck style without inspecting surrounding slides
redesigning finished slides unless asked
validation_expectations: inspect surrounding slides and at least one representative screenshot before editing or adding slides
match current spacing, title scale, footer behavior, and section visual language
use screenshots to check whether a slide is too busy or unbalanced
skills.portability_notes: Personal visual-style workflow tied to Paper deck conventions. Actual board mutation should be handled by an appropriate Paper editing workflow.

skills.name: paper-mcp
skills.purpose: Inspects and edits open Paper boards through the local Paper MCP server instead of fragile UI automation.
triggers: edit an open Paper board through the local Paper MCP server
read or write Paper canvas content, place images, write HTML, inspect nodes, take screenshots, or export through Paper MCP
avoid Computer Use for Paper board edits when MCP is reachable
do_not_use_for: Computer Use based Paper edits when the MCP server is reachable
exposing raw node IDs in final user-facing replies
deleting Paper nodes without immediate user confirmation
validation_expectations: load the Paper MCP guide once per session
inspect board info and selection before editing
verify meaningful changes with get_screenshot and finish working on nodes
skills.portability_notes: Personal tool workflow requiring a reachable local Paper MCP server and an open Paper board. The local server URL is an environment assumption, not a public service.

skills.name: prd-to-issues
skills.purpose: Converts PRDs, specs, implementation briefs, design docs, or feature plans into scoped, dependency-aware execution issues.
triggers: convert a PRD, product spec, implementation brief, design doc, feature plan, or requirements document into issues
break work into vertical slices, milestones, dependencies, or agent-ready tasks
produce JSON issue plans for Codex, GitHub issues, Linear, or harness-based execution systems
do_not_use_for: one issue per file or layer
broad polish, cleanup, or final QA issues without specific acceptance criteria
inventing features to make the plan look fuller
silently converting blocking open questions into assumptions
validation_expectations: ensure output is valid JSON
ensure dependencies reference existing issue IDs and remain acyclic
ensure each issue has acceptance criteria, verification, and realistic parallelization guidance
skills.portability_notes: Portable planning workflow. Output is a plan, not direct creation of remote GitHub or Linear issues unless another tool or user request handles that step.

skills.name: python-backend-review
skills.purpose: Reviews Python backend repositories for target-version compatibility, implementation quality, typing, async/sync correctness, configuration, logging, packaging, testing, and maintainability.
triggers: perform a version-aware Python backend fundamentals review
review Python implementation quality, type clarity, async/sync behavior, configuration, logging, packaging, or tests
assess a backend repository against a user-specified or inferred Python version
do_not_use_for: architecture review
deep agent-legibility review
framework-specific FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Pydantic, or Django review
security audit, performance deep dive, onboarding review, or business-domain review unless explicitly requested
validation_expectations: determine or state uncertainty about the target Python version
review local config, code, tests, packaging, and runtime assumptions
attempt to disprove each candidate finding before reporting
skills.portability_notes: Portable review workflow. It should not drift into architecture, framework, security, performance, or business-domain review unless the user expands scope.

skills.name: python-ecosystem-review
skills.purpose: Reviews important Python ecosystem library usage and integration boundaries against current best practices.
triggers: review Python framework, validation, ORM, migration, dependency injection, async, background job, HTTP client, testing, LLM or agent, observability, or runtime-library integration points
discover important ecosystem libraries and evaluate concrete usage
check version-sensitive library patterns with official sources when needed
do_not_use_for: broad architecture review
generic Python style review
full agent-legibility review
security or performance review
framework-only reviews unless library usage is the focus
reviewing every dependency including tooling, formatters, simple utilities, or transitive libraries
primary_outputs: version-sensitive recommendations grounded in official docs when needed
validation_expectations: discover important libraries before reviewing usage
inspect imports, setup, lifecycle hooks, schemas, repositories, clients, tasks, tests, and configuration
check official docs or changelogs for version-sensitive claims when model knowledge may be stale
skills.portability_notes: Portable review workflow, but current best-practice claims may require live official documentation checks for the target dependency versions.

skills.name: radar-analysis
skills.purpose: Analyzes existing reviewed ai-signal-radar repository history for monthly reviews, quarterly synthesis, theme analysis, trend synthesis, and idea mining.
triggers: mine AI signal radar history
summarize a month or quarter of reviewed radar records
identify recurring themes, compare periods, or draft reports from data, radars, indexes, and reports
do_not_use_for: ingesting new weekly reports
external sources or OpenAI API calls unless the user changes the contract
editing reviewed weekly source files under data or radars
creating or updating ideas files unless explicitly requested
validation_expectations: treat data JSON as primary source and radars Markdown as reviewed narrative context
run or mentally apply repository consistency checks before reporting
cite dates and file paths and separate deterministic extraction from interpretation
skills.portability_notes: Project-specific to ai-signal-radar style repositories with data, radars, indexes, themes, reports conventions, and reviewer-framing assumptions that adopters should replace with their own.

skills.name: readwise-cli-control
skills.purpose: Controls Readwise and Reader headlessly through the official CLI for search, export, organization, and automation.
triggers: search Readwise highlights or Reader documents from Codex
export or summarize saved articles, PDFs, newsletters, tweets, or highlights
organize Reader items by location, tags, shortlist, archive state, or highlights
authenticate Readwise with an access token instead of browser auth
do_not_use_for: assuming the remote MCP server accepts token-based auth
falling back to browser auth when token auth is needed
omitting original source links when Readwise has them
write operations without clear user intent
primary_outputs: JSON Readwise or Reader command results
Reader document organization changes when requested
validation_expectations: prefer readwise --json output
fetch details or export metadata when search results lack original source links
confirm intent before write operations if the user has not clearly asked to modify the library
skills.portability_notes: Tool-specific personal workflow requiring READWISE_ACCESS_TOKEN and the official Readwise CLI setup. Token values must stay in the environment or local auth, not repository files.

skills.name: tdd
skills.purpose: Guides implementation through a behavior-focused red-green-refactor loop using public interfaces and vertical slices.
triggers: build features or fix bugs using TDD
red-green-refactor
integration tests
test-first development
do_not_use_for: writing all tests first and then all implementation
implementation-coupled tests
mocking internal collaborators without a behavioral reason
speculative features beyond the current test
primary_outputs: one behavior test at a time
validation_expectations: confirm interface and behavior priorities before coding
keep each cycle to one failing test and one minimal implementation
run tests after each red-green-refactor cycle and after refactors
skills.portability_notes: Portable engineering workflow, but the public interface, test runner, and behavior priorities must come from the target project and user.

skills.name: weekly-radar-ingestion
skills.purpose: Ingests one Ray-reviewed weekly AI Systems Engineering Radar report into an ai-signal-radar repository as durable signal records and indexes.
triggers: ingest this weekly radar
add this reviewed report
import this week's signal report
store this AI radar
archive this reviewed report
turn an approved weekly AI systems report into radars, data, themes, indexes, and a commit
do_not_use_for: unreviewed draft reports
fabricating dates, themes, signals, action items, or importance
rewriting the reviewed report as a summary
adding unsupported schema fields unless the schema is intentionally updated
validation_expectations: inspect the target repository README, schemas, radar/data/theme/index structure, scripts, and git status before editing
prefer checked-in schemas and scripts when they exist
run validate_ingestion.py or repository validation and fix failures before committing
skills.portability_notes: Project-specific to Ray-reviewed AI Systems Engineering Radar ingestion and ai-signal-radar repository conventions. Adapt reviewer naming, schema fields, paths, and scripts only after inspecting the target repository contract.

skills.name: write-a-prd
skills.purpose: Turns rough ideas, feature requests, architecture proposals, product requirements, design discussions, or implementation plans into structured PRDs or specifications.
triggers: write a PRD, feature spec, requirements document, or acceptance criteria
turn an idea, vague engineering request, design discussion, or rough implementation plan into an implementation-ready brief
define scope boundaries, user workflows, non-goals, risks, and open questions
do_not_use_for: jumping directly into code
expanding scope because adjacent features are tempting
including changelogs, README text, or meta commentary in PRD output
full issue breakdown when a separate issue-breakdown workflow should handle it
validation_expectations: preserve user intent and state assumptions explicitly
make requirements specific, testable, scoped, prioritized, and traceable
label open questions as blocking or non-blocking
skills.portability_notes: Portable planning workflow. It should produce a PRD or specification, not execute implementation or issue creation unless the user separately asks.
